channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Opinion: A lesson in humanity at the Boston Marathon
item.description: Two runners in this week's Boston Marathon stopped to help a racer who had collapsed just short of the finish line. NPR's Scott Simon says their generosity is its own kind of "personal best."
item.content:encoded:
Two runners in this week's Boston Marathon stopped to help a racer who had collapsed just short of the finish line. NPR's Scott Simon says their generosity is its own kind of "personal best."

(Image credit: Cj Gunther)
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: Justice Department makes it easier to deport those with DACA status
item.description: Three appellate immigration judges sided with Department of Homeland Security lawyers who appealed a decision from Immigration Judge Michael Pleters terminating removal proceedings for DACA recipient Catalina "Xóchitl" Santiago.
item.content:encoded:
Three appellate immigration judges sided with Department of Homeland Security lawyers who appealed a decision from Immigration Judge Michael Pleters terminating removal proceedings for DACA recipient Catalina "Xóchitl" Santiago.

(Image credit: Brendan Smialowski)
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: The rising cost of fertilizer and fuel prices is pushing some farmers to the brink
item.description: In the Mississippi Delta, a crucial agricultural region, farmers say their patience is wearing thin. Reeling from the effects of tariffs, they must now also navigate rising fertilizer and fuel costs.
item.content:encoded:
In the Mississippi Delta, a crucial agricultural region, farmers say their patience is wearing thin. Reeling from the effects of tariffs, they must now also navigate rising fertilizer and fuel costs.

(Image credit: Jay Marcano for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Drew Hawkins

item.title: DOJ wants to shield its lawyers from outside scrutiny. Critics worry about oversight
item.description: Critics say the proposed rule to let the DOJ step into state bar investigations could weaken one of the last independent checks on government lawyers.
item.content:encoded:
Critics say the proposed rule to let the DOJ step into state bar investigations could weaken one of the last independent checks on government lawyers.

(Image credit: Matt McClain)
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz

item.title: Some Planned Parenthood clinics are using Botox to smooth over federal cuts
item.description: After President Trump and Congress cut certain Medicaid funding to Planned Parenthood in last year's budget, some clinics have started offering aesthetic services, including Botox, to stay afloat.
item.content:encoded:
After President Trump and Congress cut certain Medicaid funding to Planned Parenthood in last year's budget, some clinics have started offering aesthetic services, including Botox, to stay afloat.

(Image credit: Tracy Barbutes for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Laura Fitzgerald

item.title: Iran's foreign minister awaits U.S. delegation to Pakistan for peace talks
item.description: Iran's Abbas Araghchi arrived in Islamabad on Friday, as the White House confirmed Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will travel there Saturday to try to "move the ball forward towards a deal."
item.content:encoded:
Iran's Abbas Araghchi arrived in Islamabad on Friday, as the White House confirmed Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will travel there Saturday to try to "move the ball forward towards a deal."

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Justice Department to allow firing squads for executions in move to ramp up capital punishment
item.description: The Justice Department will adopt firing squad as a permitted method of execution as the Trump administration moves to ramp up and expedite capital punishment cases.
item.content:encoded:
The Justice Department will adopt firing squad as a permitted method of execution as the Trump administration moves to ramp up and expedite capital punishment cases.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Appeals court rules that Trump's asylum ban at the border is illegal
item.description: A U.S. appeals court ruled Friday that immigration laws allow people to apply for asylum at the border, and the president cannot bypass this. The decision stems from Trump declaring the border situation an invasion and suspending asylum.
item.content:encoded:
A U.S. appeals court ruled Friday that immigration laws allow people to apply for asylum at the border, and the president cannot bypass this. The decision stems from Trump declaring the border situation an invasion and suspending asylum.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: From night life in Egypt to rice farming in Vietnam, the war in Iran is a drain
item.description: Fuel costs more. Food is harder to get. Jobs are evaporating. And in Cairo, cafes and restaurants are ordered to close at 9 p.m.
item.content:encoded:
Fuel costs more. Food is harder to get. Jobs are evaporating. And in Cairo, cafes and restaurants are ordered to close at 9 p.m.

(Image credit: Ahmed Gomaa/Xinhua News Agency)
item.dc:creator: Fatma Tanis

item.title: A real-life Kraken stalked the seas of the late Cretaceous
item.description: Researchers discovered evidence of enormous Kraken-like creatures who hunted in the seas some 100 million years ago, competing with large apex predators.
item.content:encoded:
Researchers discovered evidence of enormous Kraken-like creatures who hunted in the seas some 100 million years ago, competing with large apex predators.

(Image credit: Yohei Utsuki/Department of Earth and Planetary Sciences, Hokkaido University)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel
